finding_results.notes: Eli now self-corrects and defaults to logistics under pressure (papers/money, desk and call timing), while Marisol uses concrete service-work language. Polished couplet-style exchanges were replaced with messier, task-bound dialogue in the morning and desk beats.

Mechanical echo-loop beats were replaced with context-specific pressure lines tied to wire timing, shift tasks, pain management, and missed-contact risk. Exact phrases "I hear you", "Do you?", and "Say it back" now appear zero times in the chapter.

summary: Dialogue revision separated idiolects and pressure cadence while preserving continuity, rawness, and chapter-level causal stakes.
